comment: Ground truth for the bug_hunt_codebase task. Not shown to the model.

bugs.summary: Copies the row into dead_letter but never DELETEs it from deliveries; dispatcher will keep re-picking it because delivered_at is still NULL.
Filters by deliveries.created_at instead of an attempt timestamp, so once a burst is enqueued the count never decreases.
Comment claims constant-time comparison but uses `==`; should use hmac.compare_digest. Vulnerable to timing attacks.
Function never returns anything but None because DeliveryResult exposes only response_body, not headers; Retry-After is therefore never honored.
Condition `if not is_retryable and attempts >= MAX_ATTEMPTS` requires both - so a 4xx (non-retryable) error is retried up to MAX_ATTEMPTS times instead of going to DLQ immediately.
On failure, records the attempt without setting next_scheduled_at; the row keeps scheduled_at == created_at and the dispatcher will re-attempt forever with no backoff.
Newly-inserted buckets are not move_to_end'd, so they sit at the LRU front of the OrderedDict and are evicted first by popitem(last=False). LRU semantics are inverted for new entries.

Increments only the first bucket whose upper bound >= value (uses `if value <= b: counts[i] += 1` and then keeps looping but only matches once because of how it's structured); Prometheus histograms must be cumulative across all buckets >= value. Render code reads counts[i] not the running cumulative.
No tenant-ownership check before deactivating; any authenticated tenant can deactivate any subscription if they know its UUID. Cross-tenant authorization bug.
No tenant-ownership check; an authenticated tenant can read any delivery's payload by guessing its UUID. Cross-tenant data leak.
